title: asclepias feayi
intro: Asclepias feayi, also known as the Gayflower or Gay Butterfly Weed, is a perennial plant native to the southern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its alkaloids, including cardenolides. The health benefits of Asclepias feayi include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as supporting the immune system and promoting wound healing. The plant is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and has been used in herbal remedies to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin irritations.
